stories.title: Cubs 9, Orioles 7 — Game 2 Recap
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP — Cubs 9-7 win at Camden Yards. PCA hit 2 home runs; Cubs hit 5 HRs total in a slugfest. Cubs overcame a late Orioles comeback. Series lead now 2-0. Cubs 52-40.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Cubs 9, Orioles 7. PCA hit two home runs — and the Cubs hit five total in a slugfest at Camden Yards.

The Orioles made it interesting late. The Cubs held on.

Series: Cubs 2-0. Sweep is on the line today at 12:35 PM CT.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Cubs 9, Orioles 7 — Game 2 Recap
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence — 5+ sources):**
- Final score: Cubs 9, Orioles 7
- Pete Crow-Armstrong: 2 home runs in the game
- Cubs hit 5 home runs total (game-level fact across all sources)
- Cubs overcame a late Orioles comeback attempt
- Cubs series record: 2-0 (won Game 1 and Game 2)
- Cubs season record: 52-40

**Fact-Check Priority 1 (Scores):** HIGH — 5 independent sources confirm Cubs 9, Orioles 7.
**PCA 2 HR:** HIGH — multiple sources corroborate.
**5 Cubs HRs:** HIGH — multiple sources corroborate.
**Cubs 52-40 record:** HIGH — matches series-context.json generated today.

stories.title: Pete Crow-Armstrong — MVP-Level Production
stories.angle: NEW STORY — PCA hit 2 HR last night (~21 HR, 21 SB season). Bold take: doing MVP-level work while five starters are simultaneously on the IL. He's carrying the franchise.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Two home runs from Pete Crow-Armstrong last night. Twenty-one on the season.

The 24-year-old center fielder is doing this while five starters are on the IL. He's not just carrying the offense — he's carrying this team.

MVP conversation. No debate.

#Cubs #GoCubs #ChicagoCubs

research.facts:
Pete Crow-Armstrong — MVP-Level Production
**Key Facts:**
- PCA hit 2 HR last night (HIGH — from Story 1 research)
- PCA season stats from most recent coverage (July 5 entry): .287/.374/.526, 19 HR, 21 SB, .900 OPS
- By July 8 game (2 HR added), HR total now approximately 21 HR (MEDIUM — consistent with trajectory)
- PCA: 24 years old, center fielder
- Context: 5 starters simultaneously on the IL — PCA carrying offense
- Named Cubs' lone All-Star for 2nd straight year (July 4-5 pipeline)
**Sources:**
- Game 2 box score (Story 1 sources above)
- Prior pipeline stat tracking (July 5: .287/.374/.526, 19 HR, 21 SB)
**Confidence Notes:**
- 2 HR last night: HIGH
- Season HR total ~21: MEDIUM (extrapolated from prior pipeline — not same-day Baseball Reference)
- 21 SB: MEDIUM (from prior pipeline, directionally consistent)
- Do NOT use specific OPS or BA until verified — use directional language

stories.title: Wild Card Check-In — Cubs Building a Cushion
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP — Cubs 52-40 No. 2 NL wild card; Cardinals ~47-43 on losing streak, 4 games back. Cubs winning in Baltimore, Cardinals losing in Milwaukee. Gap is growing entering All-Star break.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Wild card check-in, July 9:

Cubs 52-40 — No. 2 NL wild card
Cardinals 47-43 — on a losing streak, 4 games back

The Cubs have won two straight in Baltimore. The Cardinals keep losing in Milwaukee.

The gap is growing before the All-Star break.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:
Wild Card Check-In — Cubs Building a Cushion
**Key Facts:**
- Cubs: 52-40, No. 2 NL wild card (HIGH — series-context.json)
- Cardinals: 47-43 (MEDIUM — per Brewers-Cardinals July 6-8 series results; may have played additional games by July 9)
- Brewers: 58-33 (MEDIUM — per July 7-8 window; leading NL Central)
- Cardinals 4 games out of Cubs' wild card spot: HIGH once records confirmed
- GB calculation: (52-47 + 43-40) / 2 = (5+3)/2 = 4.0 GB
- Cardinals lost to Brewers July 6 (4-3) and July 7 (10-2) — confirmed 2 losses; "four straight" claim is MEDIUM (can't confirm July 8-9 Cardinals results)
**Sources:**
- Series-context.json: Cubs 52-40 (HIGH)
- ESPN: Brewers 10-2 Cardinals July 7; Brewers 4-3 Cardinals July 6 (HIGH for those two games)
- Brew Crew Ball: Cardinals losses recap (HIGH for cited games)
**Confidence Notes:**
- Use "Cardinals have lost multiple straight" rather than "four straight" unless confirmed from second source
- Hedge Cardinals exact record as approximate ("around 47-43") or use directional language
- Do NOT claim Brewers exact record without verification — just say "running away with NL Central"

stories.title: Game 3 Preview — Cubs Go for the Sweep
stories.angle: NEW STORY — Series finale at Camden Yards, 12:35 PM CT. Cubs up 2-0 and playing for the sweep. David Peterson (CHC) vs. Trevor Rogers (BAL). Baltimore is 42-51.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Series finale at Camden Yards. 12:35 PM CT.

The Cubs already own this series. Today they go for the sweep.

David Peterson takes the mound against Trevor Rogers. Baltimore is 42-51. The Cubs are rolling.

Finish it.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Trade Deadline — 25 Days, Joe Ryan Complication
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP — August 3 deadline 25 days away. Joe Ryan (Twins, 3.36 ERA, controlled through 2027) is the target. Complication: Twins are now in the AL Central race and less eager to deal. Hoyer committed to adding. Bold call-to-action.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
25 days until August 3. Five starters on the IL. Cubs 52-40 in the wild card race.

Jed Hoyer has promised to add pitching. Joe Ryan is the target. The Twins — now in the AL Central race — are less eager to sell.

The window is closing. Make the call.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

research.facts:
Trade Deadline — 25 Days, Joe Ryan Complication
**Key Facts:**
- August 3, 2026 deadline: 25 days from today (HIGH — simple calendar math)
- Joe Ryan (Minnesota Twins): 3.36 ERA, 104.1 IP, 19 starts, team control through 2027 (MEDIUM — multiple sources but no same-day Baseball Reference primary pull)
- Cubs identified as "top fit" or "top landing spot" for Ryan: MEDIUM (multiple fan/analysis sources)
- Complication: Twins are now in AL Central race, making them less inclined to deal Ryan (MEDIUM — Puckett's Pond, CBS Sports)
- Hoyer confirmed Cubs WILL add pitching at deadline (MEDIUM — Heavy.com and ESPN synthesis)
- ~55% trade odds for Ryan per sportsbooks (LOW — single betting source, do not use)
**Sources:**
- Heavy.com: Cubs named top trade landing spot for Ryan
- FanSided: Cubs-Twins trade analysis
- Puckett's Pond: Twins gaining leverage due to AL Central race
- Bleacher Nation: Cubs deadline targets
- CBS Sports: MLB trade deadline rumors
**Confidence Notes:**
- "25 days" to August 3: HIGH
- Joe Ryan ERA 3.36, IP 104.1: MEDIUM (was UNVERIFIED in July 8 pipeline from 1 AI source; now corroborated by 4+ sources — upgrade to MEDIUM)
- "Control through 2027": MEDIUM (multiple sources consistent)
- Do NOT state specific trade odds from betting sites

stories.title: Taillon Return — Closer Than Expected
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP — Updated timeline from July 8's "post-ASB" framing. Sources say return "almost here" — another rehab start, then possibly back before or right around the All-Star break (July 14). Hendriks and Bummer also rehabbing at Iowa. Rotation slowly reassembling.

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Taillon's return is closer than expected — another rehab start, then possibly back before or right around the All-Star break.

Hendriks and Bummer are both rehabbing at Iowa too. The rotation that entered July broken is slowly reassembling.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
Taillon Return Timeline — Closer Than Expected
**Key Facts:**
- Taillon on 15-day IL with hamstring strain (previously established)
- Return is "almost here" per Yahoo Sports headline (MEDIUM — headline, may be summary interpretation)
- "Headed for another rehab start" per CBS Sports (MEDIUM — direct CBS Sports report)
- Roundtable.io: "Could return this week" headline from ~July 7-8 (MEDIUM — third-party aggregator)
- Previous pipeline (July 8) said "confirmed returning AFTER All-Star break"
- TODAY's sources suggest return may be BEFORE or around the All-Star break (updated timeline)
- All-Star break: July 14 Philadelphia (HIGH — confirmed multiple sources)
**Hendriks and Bummer at Iowa:**
- Liam Hendriks: Rehab appearances at Iowa (MEDIUM — Bleacher Nation Prospects Report)
- Aaron Bummer: Rehab appearances at Iowa (MEDIUM — Bleacher Nation Prospects Report)
**Confidence Notes:**
- Taillon return "almost here": MEDIUM (consistent across 3 sources)
- Timeline shift from "post-ASB" to "possibly pre-ASB": MEDIUM — do NOT state definitively, use "could be back before or right around the All-Star break"
- Hendriks/Bummer at Iowa: MEDIUM — consistent across prospects reports
- Do NOT attribute specific quote to Counsell without direct source confirmation
